response.justification: The candidate has extensive experience as a senior full-stack and backend software engineer, but fundamentally lacks the hardware engineering and AI accelerator background required for this role. There is no evidence of SoC/IP modeling, hardware-software co-design, or deep-learning workload optimization at the infrastructure level. While highly skilled in web and microservices development, their profile does not align with a Principal Performance Architect position.
strengths: Over 12 years of senior-level software engineering experience across full-stack, backend, and mobile development.
Proven ability to build low-latency, high-performance microservices using languages like DLang and C/C++.
Strong track record of technical leadership, open-source contribution, and mentoring through workshops.
concerns: Complete lack of experience with System-on-Chip (SoC) and IP modeling or hardware-software co-design.
No background in GPU architecture, SIMD/MIMD, or hardware power-optimization trade-offs.
AI experience is limited to high-level LLM tooling rather than deep neural network training and inference optimization.
